note: chunk-parallel gated delta rule, C=64 (batched matmuls + unit-triangular solve for net-writes, carry S between blocks)
chunk-parallel C=64 + torch.compile(mode=reduce-overhead) on the short outer chunk loop (CUDA-graph capture absorbs per-chunk launch overhead)
chunk size C=64->128 (swept {32,64,128,256} privately; C=128 sweet spot: bigger matmuls better GPU occupancy, C=256 hurts on the larger triangular solve)

fused intra-chunk matmuls: cat([S,kDn^T]) shared by kUp/qUp matmuls + cat([A,kDn^T])@U for term2/W (6 bmms->3). correct but REGRESSED eval (cat allocs + bigger matmuls cost > launch savings under CUDA graphs)
W einsum->bmm (cuBLAS+TF32); A/term2 kept einsum (their shared-operand needs 268MB expand if bmm'd). Neutral: confirms einsum already lowers to cuBLAS bmm. [diagnostic: trsm is ~22% of runtime]

compile mode reduce-overhead->max-autotune (inductor autotunes gemm+epilogue fusion, no hand-written Triton). train +5.7%; eval ~neutral. [diag: term2 einsums=33% runtime but parallel-gemm-bound; Triton serial-scan & Zt-prefix both worse]
inductor config: coordinate_descent_tuning=True + max_autotune_pointwise=True on top of max-autotune (finer epilogue/reduction tuning). train +1%; eval ~neutral, eval compile 115s->80s.

share qUp_flat left factor between term1 (qUp@S) and A (qUp@kDn^T) gemms; einsum->bmm for term2. Fresh diag: term1=14%, term2=33% of runtime, both qUp-matmuls -> inductor fuses the shared left-factor read. train +1.7%.

compile mode max-autotune->max-autotune-no-cudagraphs. Last turn found bwd=72% of fb (large sequential bwd graph); dropping cudagraph capture lets inductor fuse the bwd more aggressively. train +6%, eval +19%.

bf16 gemms (cuBLAS bf16, fp32 internal accum) for m_inter/term1/Kmat/A/term2/W; S stays fp32 (no compounding). Doubles gemm throughput on compute-bound fwd+bwd. fwd relMSE 1.8e-7->1.44e-5, grad 8.7e-7->1.02e-4 (~100x worse but ~100x under tol).

Triton batched unit-lower trsm (rank-1-update fwd-sub) replaces cuSOLVER solve_triangular; custom autograd.Function with hand bwd (L^T transpose solve + masked g_L). trsm was 23% of fb (differential-measured). Boundary is free (trsm was already opaque). Triton trsm 24% faster than cuSOLVER in isolation (0.069 vs 0.091ms), relMSE 4.6e-14. S stays fp32.

Triton unit-UPPER trsm (backward-sub rank-1) replaces cuSOLVER L^T transpose solve in _TrsmFn.backward. Both trsm fwd+bwd now Triton. bwd-trsm+g_L bmm was 11% of fb (differential-measured). Triton upper trsm 22% faster than cuSOLVER in isolation (0.065 vs 0.083ms), relMSE 4.6e-14. S stays fp32.

Triton trsm tiling sweep: BLOCK_DV 64->32, num_warps 4->2 on BOTH trsm kernels. Isolated sweep showed BDV=32/nw=2 best (lower 0.054 vs 0.064ms -16%; upper 0.041 vs 0.067ms -39%); smaller tile = more parallelism (4 progs/batch) + cheaper masked row-extract; BDV=128 was worse (masked-sum over 128x128 tile too big). relMSE 4.6e-14 unchanged. trsms were 19% of fb.
REPAIRED by hand: C=128->16 for fp32 gcumF safety + fp32 decay-ratio gemms; bf16 kept on S-path; default torch.compile.
